2605.27315.title: Real Images, Worse Judgments: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Concreteness and Imagery
2605.27315.paper_summary: Visual inputs are often assumed to improve language understanding in multimodal models. We examine this assumption by asking whether vision-language models (VLMs) can distinguish useful visual evidence from incidental image context in lexical judgments. We use human concreteness and imagery ratings because they span words with varying expected visual relevance, from abstract and low-imagery words to concrete and high-imagery words. We find that real-image contexts do not yield consistent gains and often hurt alignment with human ratings, most sharply when visual evidence is least relevant. Through probing and canonical correlation analysis, complemented by an attribution case study, we find that real-image contexts are associated with representational shifts and greater sensitivity to spurious visual cues, coinciding with weaker recoverability of the targeted lexical properties. We further show that instructing models to focus solely on textual content at inference time can reduce this degradation, with the clearest gains on these vulnerable subsets. Our findings suggest that current instruction-tuned VLMs need better calibration of when visual context should inform lexical judgments.
2605.27315.paper_summary_zh: 視覺輸入通常被假設能改善多模態模型中的語言理解。我們通過詢問視覺-語言模型（VLMs）是否能在詞彙判斷中區分有用的視覺證據與偶然的圖像背景來檢驗這一假設。我們使用人類的具體性和意象評分，因為它們涵蓋了從抽象和低意象詞到具體和高意象詞的不同預期視覺相關性。我們發現，真實圖像背景並未帶來一致的增益，並且通常會損害與人類評分的對齊，尤其是在視覺證據最不相關時。通過探測和典範相關性分析，輔以一個歸因案例研究，我們發現真實圖像背景與表徵轉變和對虛假視覺線索的更大敏感性相關，這與目標詞彙特性的可恢復性較弱相一致。我們進一步表明，在推理時指示模型僅專注於文本內容可以減少這種退化，對這些脆弱子集的增益最為明顯。我們的發現表明，目前的指令調整VLMs需要更好地校準何時視覺背景應該影響詞彙判斷。
2605.27315.author: Yifan Jiang et.al.
2605.27315.authors: Yifan Jiang, Ruoxi Ning, Sheng Yao, Freda Shi
